title: Monitoring of Torque Teno virus DNAemia in critically ill COVID-19 patients: May it help to predict clinical outcomes?
author: Carbonell-Monleón, Nieves
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This study analyzes whether the DNA load of the Torque Teno Virus (TTV) in plasma can predict nosocomial infections and mortality in critically ill COVID-19 patients. In 50 ICU patients, TTV DNA was quantified using real-time PCR, and inflammatory and tissue damage biomarkers were evaluated. TTV was detected in 76% of cases, with a weak inverse correlation with lymphocyte count. The results suggest that a higher TTV load in plasma is associated with a greater risk of nosocomial infections and a trend toward higher mortality, highlighting its potential as a biomarker of immunosuppression in severe COVID-19
